response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger allergic reactions in some people  
allergic reactions can cause symptoms such as difficulty breathing and swelling of the throat  
these reactions can be severe and life-threatening  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot eat them and must avoid them in all forms  
underwater environments do not contain peanuts and are not a source of peanut exposure  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because they are not exposed to peanuts in such environments  

Sec. 2  
the absence of peanuts in underwater environments means there is no risk of allergic reactions  
this makes it safe for people with peanut allergies to be in underwater environments  
the primary concern for peanut allergies is exposure to peanuts not in the form of food  
underwater environments do not pose a risk to people with peanut allergies  
this is different from other allergens that can be present in various environments
